perturbation.original_text: 1.26 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means: (a) with respect to the efforts to be expended, or considerations to be undertaken, by a Party or its Affiliate with respect to any objective, activity or decision to be undertaken hereunder, reasonable, good faith efforts to accomplish such objective, activity or decision as such Party would normally use to accomplish a similar objective, activity or decision under similar circumstances; and (b) with respect to Development and Commercialization of any Licensed Product for any indication by a Party, efforts and resources consistent with those efforts and resources commonly used by a similarly situated biotechnology company with respect to a product owned by it or to which it has similar rights, which product is at a similar stage in its development or product life and is of similar market potential taking into account (i) the patent and other proprietary position of the Licensed Product and (ii) the anticipated profitability of the Licensed Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.26 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" or 'CRE' means: (a) with respect to the efforts to be expended, or considerations to be undertaken, by a Party or its Affiliate with respect to any objective, activity or decision to be undertaken hereunder, sincere efforts reflecting a genuine commitment to achieve the desired outcome, as determined by the Party in its sole discretion; and (b) with respect to Development and Commercialization of any Licensed Product for any indication by a Party, minimal efforts and resources reasonably expected to produce a profit, irrespective of industry standards or customary practices. The 'CRE' should not be interpreted as an obligation to invest beyond what is financially advantageous.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' based on industry standards and similar biotechnology companies. The modified version, however, introduces a subjective standard ('sincere efforts' as determined by the party) and contradicts this by specifying 'minimal efforts' based on expected profit. This creates uncertainty and contradiction because one definition states that there should be 'sincere efforts', which is based on good faith and another part of the same definition states that the 'CRE' should be interpreted to generate a profit, which makes the definition uncertain. Additionally, the added acronym ('CRE') isn't used in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 General. Vyera shall have the exclusive right to implement, and subject to Section 5.5, final decision-making authority with respect to, Commercialization of all Licensed Products in the Field and the Territory. Vyera shall be solely and exclusively responsible for all costs and expenses associated with Commercialization of Licensed Products in the Field and the Territory. Vyera shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts in connection with such Commercialization of Licensed Products in the Territory for each indication in the Field for which such Licensed Products have received Regulatory Approval, and shall conduct Commercialization activities in material compliance with Applicable Laws and shall ensure that its Third Party contractors conduct Commercialization activities in material compliance with Applicable Laws. Without limiting the foregoing, Vyera shall have the exclusive right and responsibility throughout the Territory for the following: (a) receiving and accepting orders for the Licensed Product from customers; (b) distributing the Licensed Product to customers; (c) controlling invoicing and collection of accounts receivable for Licensed Product sales; (d) recording Licensed Product sales in its books of account for sales (in accordance with Vyera's accounting standards consistently applied (currently GAAP)); (e) subject to Section 5.5, determining pricing for the Licensed Product and all aspects of the promotion (including promotional materials) to be used in Commercializing Licensed Products; (f) negotiating with Third Parties, including without limitation, payors, pharmacy benefit managers and distributors, with respect to sales and distribution of Licensed Product; and (g) paying all rebates, chargebacks and other amounts due to customers in respect of Licensed Products (it being understood that all such amounts shall be deducted in calculating Net Sales). Notwithstanding the foregoing, CytoDyn acknowledges and agrees that in the event Vyera delivers to CytoDyn a notice of termination pursuant to Sections 11.2(b) or (c), the use of Commercially Reasonable Efforts shall take into account Vyera's intent to cease its Commercialization activities as of the end of the applicable notice period and shall not require Vyera to take any action that is inconsistent with such intent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' (CRE) to include a subjective standard determined by the party in its sole discretion and introduces the concept of 'minimal efforts' based on expected profit, irrespective of industry standards. This directly contradicts section 5.1, which mandates that Vyera 'shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts in connection with such Commercialization of Licensed Products'. The original definition aligned with industry standards, while the changed definition allows for a lower standard of effort based on profit, effectively undermining the obligation in section 5.1. This creates a strong contradiction because section 5.1 would expect Vyera to exert a reasonable amount of effort and the definition is now based on what is financially advantageous.

perturbation.original_text: 1.70 "Licensed Product" means any pharmaceutical product, including all forms, presentations, strengths, doses and formulations (including any method of delivery), that contains leronlimab (PRO-140) as defined by [***] that CytoDyn is currently evaluating in its clinical development program for the treatment of HIV infection that is Covered by one or more claims of a CytoDyn Patent or CytoDyn Know-How.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.70 "Licensed Product" or 'Product' means only the initial formulation of leronlimab (PRO-140) as it exists on the Effective Date, specifically excluding any future improvements, modifications, or new formulations developed by CytoDyn, regardless of patent coverage or clinical evaluation.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Licensed Product' is broad, encompassing all forms and future presentations of leronlimab covered by CytoDyn's patents or know-how. The modified definition severely restricts this, limiting it to the initial formulation only and explicitly excluding future developments. This contradicts the original intention of the contract, which anticipated future developments would also be licensed, creating a contradiction on the text in multiple sections where the future formulations of the 'Licensed Product' are being mentioned. Furthermore, the change in definition introduces ambiguity and uncertainty in defining future versions of the leronlimab.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Development Plan and Development Activities. CytoDyn shall have sole responsibility for, and final decision-making authority with respect to, performance of Development of the Licensed Product for the Initial Indication and any Subsequent Indication. CytoDyn shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to execute and perform the activities set forth in the Development Plan. CytoDyn shall conduct the activities under the Development Plan, and shall ensure that its Affiliates and contractors conduct their activities under the Development Plan, in a good scientific manner and in material compliance with Applicable Law, including cGLP, cGCP, cGMP and applicable national and international guidelines. For clarity, the Development Plan will only include activities related to indications in the Field.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Licensed Product' broadly, including all forms and future presentations of leronlimab. The changed text narrows the definition to only the initial formulation, excluding future developments. This directly contradicts section 4.1, which discusses the 'Development Plan' for the 'Licensed Product' including any 'Subsequent Indication', implying future development and formulations are covered. By excluding future formulations, the changed definition makes the Development Plan in section 4.1 contradictory and potentially unenforceable with respect to any new versions of leronlimab.

perturbation.original_text: 1.66 "Knowledge" means, (a) with respect to CytoDyn, the actual knowledge (following due inquiry) of Nader Z. Pourhassan, Michael D. Mulholland, Nitya G. Ray, and Brendan Rae, and (b) with respect to Vyera, the actual knowledge (following due inquiry) of Averill L. Powers, Ruchin Patel, Nicholas J. Pelliccione and Anne K. Kirby.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.66 "Knowledge" means, (a) with respect to CytoDyn, the deemed knowledge of CytoDyn's CEO, regardless of actual awareness or due inquiry, and (b) with respect to Vyera, the collective knowledge of all Vyera employees and consultants, without any obligation to conduct due inquiry.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Knowledge' specifies certain individuals whose actual knowledge, after due inquiry, is attributed to each party. The modified definition changes this dramatically: for CytoDyn, it shifts to 'deemed knowledge' of the CEO regardless of actual awareness, which removes the 'due inquiry' aspect, and for Vyera, it expands to encompass the 'collective knowledge' of all employees and consultants without any requirement for 'due inquiry'. This discrepancy in Knowledge means that depending on the individual or party that's being enquired or have committed an action there would be a contradiction. This affects representations and warranties within the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) To CytoDyn's Knowledge as of the Effective Date and without any additional independent investigation by its outside patent counsel other than such freedom to operate analysis as have previously been performed and shared with CytoDyn, the Commercialization of the Licensed Product in the Field in the Territory will not infringe or misappropriate the Patents or other intellectual property or proprietary rights of any Third Party in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Knowledge" significantly alters the scope and nature of knowledge attributed to each party. The original definition relies on the 'actual knowledge (following due inquiry)' of specific individuals. The changed definition shifts this to 'deemed knowledge' regardless of actual awareness for CytoDyn and the 'collective knowledge' without due inquiry for Vyera. This directly contradicts section (f), which refers to 'CytoDyn's Knowledge as of the Effective Date' and suggests a limited scope of inquiry ('without any additional independent investigation'). Because the definition of Knowledge changes, this can now reasonably include the knowledge of all CytoDyn employees, fundamentally changing the scope of what CytoDyn is warranting.
